updated: 2026-07-06 (post-round: real read-only NAV client build, Entra auth, off DATA_SOURCES.md; data path corrected from NAV login to Azure SQL + Entra)

stack.decision: Option B: Node + TypeScript backend, React + TypeScript frontend

gates.devops_provisioning: clarified 2026-07-06 via docs/DATA_SOURCES.md: NAV is Azure SQL (sql-grus-prd-01, db sqldb-nav18-grus-prd-01), Entra auth (no passwords). Ask: grant the dashboard a read-only Entra identity (managed identity or service principal) with db_datareader. Local dev already works via az login. Middleware base URL (middleware.grundens.com, Cloudflare-fronted, dashboard.rs endpoints unauthenticated) still to confirm for reachability.
gates.ba_orphan_disambiguation: likely resolved, pending Mari: GRUS$Sales Header.WebOrder (tinyint) disambiguates (WebOrder=1 + empty WebId = orphan; WebOrder=0 = never orphan). Client wired to read WebOrder; ORDER_ORPHAN_GRADING_ENABLED stays off until Mari confirms, then it is a config flip.
gates.ba_nav_codeunit_schema: open: blocks any unit reading it; ship additively without that source
gates.ux_signoff: signed off on the Unit 1 reference panel (2026-07-06); panels replicate that template
gates.deploy_target: decided 2026-07-06: ADR-0005 accepted Alternative B (cloud VM + owned monitored VPN); CD pipeline built (Unit 10). Fallback A if the VPN cannot be made reliable.
gates.remaining_after_round: DevOps provisioning is the only open work: stand up the cloud VM, the owned VPN with line-of-sight to internal NAV + middleware, create the GitHub production environment with required reviewers, and set the DEPLOY_SSH_* + DATABASE_URL + MIDDLEWARE_* + NAV_* secrets. Then set AGGREGATOR_ENABLED=true to run against real sources. BA questions (orphan grading, NAV codeunit schema) remain open and are shipped additively.
